idea.raw_input: Fix the add transaction screen - transactions are not working properly

overview.problem: The Add Transaction screen has 3 bugs: (1) addTransaction() doesn't await _saveTransactions() so transactions are lost on app restart, (2) category names in AddTransactionScreen don't match BudgetModel/BudgetGoalsModel categorization names causing all transactions to land in 'Other', (3) BudgetGoalsModel._categorize() doesn't recognize keywords like 'health', 'subs' used by the add screen.
overview.solution: Fix addTransaction to be async and await save. Map add screen category names to the correct BudgetGoalsModel category names. Expand _categorize() keyword matching in BudgetGoalsModel.
overview.target_user: Fortuna budget app users who need transactions to persist and be correctly categorized.
overview.success_metric: Transactions persist after app restart and are categorized correctly (not all 'Other').

screens.name: AddTransactionScreen
screens.purpose: Allow users to add expense/income transactions with correct category mapping.

features.name: Fix async transaction persistence
features.description: Change addTransaction() to return Future<void> and await _saveTransactions(). Update _submitTransaction() in AddTransactionScreen to await the call.
acceptance_criteria: Given a transaction is added, when the app is restarted, then the transaction persists in SharedPreferences.
Given addTransaction is called, when it completes, then _saveTransactions has finished writing.

features.name: Fix category name mapping
features.description: AddTransactionScreen category names must map to BudgetGoalsModel category names so _categorize() works correctly.
acceptance_criteria: Given user selects 'Groceries' on add screen, when transaction is saved, then it is categorized as 'Food & Dining'.
Given user selects 'Rent', when transaction is saved, then it is categorized as 'Bills'.
Given user selects 'Subs', when transaction is saved, then it is categorized as 'Entertainment'.

features.name: Expand BudgetGoalsModel categorization keywords
features.description: Add keyword matching for 'health', 'subscription', 'sub', 'pharmacy', 'medical' in BudgetGoalsModel._categorize().
acceptance_criteria: Given a transaction with description containing 'health', when categorized, then it maps to a non-'Other' category.
Given a transaction with description containing 'subscription' or 'sub', when categorized, then it maps to 'Entertainment'.

data.backend_notes: All data from local SharedPreferences via BudgetModel.

state_management.justification: Existing app uses Provider. No need to change state management for bug fixes.

navigation.justification: Existing app uses go_router with ShellRoute for tab navigation.

local_storage.justification: Existing app uses SharedPreferences for local transaction storage.

observability.justification: Small app, no remote observability needed.

tickets.title: Fix BudgetModel.addTransaction to await async save
tickets.description: Change addTransaction() from void to Future<void> and await _saveTransactions().
acceptance_criteria: addTransaction() returns Future<void> instead of void
tickets.coder_notes: Change void to Future<void> and add await.

tickets.title: Fix AddTransactionScreen category name mapping
tickets.description: Add display-name-to-category-name mapping and make _submitTransaction async.
tickets.coder_notes: Add categoryName field to _CategoryInfo, use firstWhere to look up mapped name.

tickets.title: Expand BudgetGoalsModel._categorize() keyword matching
tickets.description: Add keyword matching for health, subscription, sub, pharmacy, medical.
tickets.coder_notes: Add conditions before final Other fallback.

criteria_results.criterion: addTransaction() returns Future<void> instead of void
